response.justification: The candidate is a capable, versatile senior engineer with a strong web and full-stack background, but he misses nearly every hard requirement for this role. There is no Japanese proficiency listed (native-level is required), no Linux kernel analysis or modification experience, no L2/L3 switch or embedded networking background, and no exposure to the required security technologies such as MACsec or IEEE 802.1X. His C experience is real but brief and dated (2014-2015), while the bulk of his career is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang product work. He is a strong engineer for a different job, not this one.
strengths: Broad, proven engineering range across web, mobile, desktop and some embedded/hardware work (robotic arms, audio systems, low-latency services)
Genuine C/C++ and Linux exposure, including C IDE components at National Instruments and systems-level DLang work
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and active knowledge sharing
concerns: No Japanese language ability listed, which fails the native-level Japanese hard requirement outright
No experience with Linux kernel source modification, switch/networking stacks, or the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal)
C-for-UNIX experience is limited to a short stint a decade ago; recent work is almost entirely web-focused TypeScript/Node.js

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
Solid general engineering foundation with some C/C++ and Linux
